# **Olist E-Commerce: Delivery & Customer Satisfaction Analysis (SQL)**

**Tools:** BigQuery (SQL) · Excel (visualization)  
**Dataset:** [Olist Brazilian E-Commerce](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/olistbr/brazilian-ecommerce) 

---

## **Question to Answer**

**How much does the delivery speed impact customer satisfaction and what is the desired threshold to overcome?**

---

## **Key Findings**

### **1\. Shipping Speed Drives Review Scores**

Customers who leave 5-star reviews receive their orders **twice as fast** as 1-star reviewers.

| Review Score | % of Reviews | Avg. Shipping Days | Days Ahead of Estimate |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **5** ★ | 59% | 10.6 | 13.4 |
| **4** ★ | 18% | 12.3 | 12.4 |
| **3**★ | 8% | 14.2 | 10.8 |
| **2**★ | 3% | 16.6 | 8.6 |
| **1** ★ | 10% | 21.3 | 4.0 |

 **Action:** Set shipping target to be ≤11 days. Each day above that measurably decreases customer satisfaction

![][image1]  
---

### **2\. Missing the Estimated Date Cuts Satisfaction in Half**

The single most impactful threshold is whether an order arrives by the promised date.

| Delivery Status | % of Orders | Avg. Review Score |
| :---: | :---: | :---: |
|  In-Time | 93.35% | 4.29 |
|  Overdue | 6.65% | 2.27 |

 **Action:** Prioritize logistics to deliver orders In-Time to get \~2 points of customer satisfaction

---

### **3\. Over-Delivery Is the Secret to 5-Star Reviews**

59% of all reviews are 5 stars, largely because customers receive orders **13+ days ahead of the estimate**. The buffer between actual and estimated delivery is the strongest predictor of top scores.

 **Action:** Aim for higher delivery time estimates. Under-promising and over-delivering is low-cost and high-impact.

---

## **SQL Approach**

Both queries use the same 2 CTEs. The only that differ is the final SELECT:

Base(same for both): First CTE calculates delivery time metrics, then second CTE joins reviews and classifies if the delivery is Overdue or In-Time. Then:

1. *Delivery Time Query* — computes actual shipping days, estimated days, difference between them and groups by rating.(For delivered orders only)

2. *Delivery Groups Query*— computes average score and groups by deliveries that were delivered In-Time and by deliveries that were Overdue. 

See [`queries/`](https://github.com/Kobesc/SQL_olist_delivery_analysis/blob/402a1737eee10bf463d96fa094db9ad251ce44ac/SQL_queries.pdf) for the full SQL.

---

##  **Business Impact Summary**

| Lever | Current State | Opportunity |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Shipping speed | Avg 10.6 days for 5★ | Reduce tail (1★ avg \= 21.3 days) |
| On-time delivery | 93% in-time | Protect — each miss costs \~2 score points |
| Estimate setting | 13+ day buffer for 5★ | Widen estimates to manufacture "early" arrivals  |

# Key Actions to Take

* #### **Give greater delivery time estimates**

  #### 

* #### **Aim for delivery time under 11 days**
